outgoing_links.text: Breathe on Me, Breath of God

outgoing_links.text: Der Geist des Herrn erfüllt das All
outgoing_links.target_title: Der Geist des Herrn erfüllt das All

outgoing_links.text: Komm, Heilger Geist, der Leben schafft
outgoing_links.target_title: Komm, Heilger Geist, der Leben schafft

outgoing_links.text: Komm, Schöpfer Geist, kehr bei uns ein

outgoing_links.text: Nun bitten wir den Heiligen Geist

outgoing_links.text: O komm, du Geist der Wahrheit

| [Autocephalous](/wiki/Autocephaly "Autocephaly") jurisdictions  Autocephalous Churches who are officially part of the communion: * [Constantinople](/wiki/Ecumenical_Patriarchate_of_Constantinople "Ecumenical Patriarchate of Constantinople") * [Alexandria](/wiki/Greek_Orthodox_Patriarchate_of_Alexandria "Greek Orthodox Patriarchate of Alexandria") * [Antioch](/wiki/Greek_Orthodox_Patriarchate_of_Antioch "Greek Orthodox Patriarchate of Antioch") * [Jerusalem](/wiki/Greek_Orthodox_Patriarchate_of_Jerusalem "Greek Orthodox Patriarchate of Jerusalem") * [Russia](/wiki/Russian_Orthodox_Church "Russian Orthodox Church") * [Serbia](/wiki/Serbian_Orthodox_Church "Serbian Orthodox Church") * [Romania](/wiki/Romanian_Orthodox_Church "Romanian Orthodox Church") * [Bulgaria](/wiki/Bulgarian_Orthodox_Church "Bulgarian Orthodox Church") * [Georgia](/wiki/Georgian_Orthodox_Church "Georgian Orthodox Church") * [Cyprus](/wiki/Church_of_Cyprus "Church of Cyprus") * [Greece](/wiki/Church_of_Greece "Church of Greece") * [Poland](/wiki/Polish_Orthodox_Church "Polish Orthodox Church") * [Albania](/wiki/Albanian_Orthodox_Church "Albanian Orthodox Church") * [Czech Lands and Slovakia](/wiki/Orthodox_Church_of_the_Czech_Lands_and_Slovakia "Orthodox Church of the Czech Lands and Slovakia")  Autocephaly recognized by some autocephalous Churches *de jure*:  * [America](/wiki/Orthodox_Church_in_America "Orthodox Church in America") * [North Macedonia](/wiki/Macedonian_Orthodox_Church "Macedonian Orthodox Church")  Autocephaly and canonicity recognized by Constantinople and 3 other autocephalous Churches:  * [Ukraine (OCU)](/wiki/Orthodox_Church_of_Ukraine "Orthodox Church of Ukraine")   Spiritual independence recognized by Georgian Orthodox Church:  * [Ukraine (UOC)](/wiki/Ukrainian_Orthodox_Church_(Moscow_Patriarchate) "Ukrainian Orthodox Church (Moscow Patriarchate)") |

The **Apostles' Fast**, also called the **Fast of the Holy Apostles**, the **Fast of Peter and Paul**, or sometimes **St. Peter's Fast**,[[1]](#cite_note-1) is a [fast](/wiki/Fasting "Fasting") observed by [Eastern Orthodox](/wiki/Eastern_Orthodox "Eastern Orthodox"), [Oriental Orthodox](/wiki/Oriental_Orthodox "Oriental Orthodox"), [Eastern Catholic](/wiki/Eastern_Catholic "Eastern Catholic"), and [Eastern Protestant](/wiki/Reformed_Orthodoxy_(Eastern_Christianity) "Reformed Orthodoxy (Eastern Christianity)") Christians. In the Byzantine tradition, the Fast begins on the second Monday after [Pentecost](/wiki/Pentecost "Pentecost") (the day after [All Saints' Sunday](/wiki/Trinity_Sunday "Trinity Sunday")), whereas in the Coptic and old Syriac traditions, the Fast begins on the first Monday after Pentecost.

It continues until the [Feast of Saints Peter and Paul](/wiki/Feast_of_Saints_Peter_and_Paul "Feast of Saints Peter and Paul") on [June 29](/wiki/June_29_(Eastern_Orthodox_liturgics) "June 29 (Eastern Orthodox liturgics)"). Traditionally, its duration varies from eight to forty-two days because of the [moveable](/wiki/Moveable_feast "Moveable feast") nature of [Pascha](/wiki/Easter "Easter"). However, in Eastern Orthodox churches that follow the [Revised Julian calendar](/wiki/Revised_Julian_calendar "Revised Julian calendar"), the fast can be as long as 29 days, or may not occur at all in some years.

## History

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Apostles%27_Fast&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: History")]

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/c/cb/Peter_and_Paul_icon_Belozersk.jpg/250px-Peter_and_Paul_icon_Belozersk.jpg)](/wiki/File:Peter_and_Paul_icon_Belozersk.jpg)

13th century icon of Ss. Peter and Paul ([Belozersk](/wiki/Belozersk "Belozersk")).

Having rejoiced for fifty days following [Pascha](/wiki/Easter "Easter") (Easter), the [Resurrection of Jesus Christ](/wiki/Resurrection_of_Jesus_Christ "Resurrection of Jesus Christ"), the [Apostles](/wiki/The_Twelve_Apostles/Disciples "The Twelve Apostles/Disciples") began to prepare for their departure from [Jerusalem](/wiki/Early_centers_of_Christianity#Jerusalem "Early centers of Christianity") to spread Christ's message. According to [Sacred Tradition](/wiki/Sacred_Tradition "Sacred Tradition"), as part of their preparation, they began a fast with prayer to ask God to strengthen their resolve and to be with them in their [missionary](/wiki/Missionary "Missionary") undertakings.

The scriptural foundation for the Fast is found in the [Synoptic Gospels](/wiki/Synoptic_Gospels "Synoptic Gospels"), when the [Pharisees](/wiki/Pharisees "Pharisees") criticized the apostles for not fasting, Jesus said to them, "Can the children of the bridechamber mourn, as long as the bridegroom is with them? But the days will come, when the bridegroom shall be taken from them, and then shall they fast."[[2]](#cite_note-2) In the immediate sense, Christ was referring to his being taken to be [crucified](/wiki/Passion_(Christianity) "Passion (Christianity)"); but in the wider sense it is understood in terms of his [Ascension](/wiki/Ascension_of_Jesus_Christ "Ascension of Jesus Christ") into heaven and his [commission](/wiki/Great_Commission "Great Commission") to preach the [Gospel](/wiki/Gospel "Gospel"), which can only be accomplished with prayer and fasting.

The Fast is thought to have been instituted out of thanksgiving to God for the witness of the apostles of Christ. With this Fast, believers express their thanks for the apostles' endurance of persecution during their mission.

## Practice

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Apostles%27_Fast&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Practice")]

The Apostles Fast is not as strict as [Great Lent](/wiki/Great_Lent "Great Lent") or the [Dormition Fast](/wiki/Dormition_of_the_Theotokos "Dormition of the Theotokos"), but entails [fasting](/wiki/Eastern_Orthodoxy#Fasting "Eastern Orthodoxy") from [red meat](/wiki/Red_meat "Red meat"), [poultry](/wiki/Poultry "Poultry"), [meat products](/wiki/Broth "Broth"), eggs, [dairy products](/wiki/Dairy "Dairy"), fish, oil, and wine. For many Orthodox, fish, wine, and oil are allowed on all days except Wednesdays and Fridays.[[3]](#cite_note-3) Some other Orthodox, such as the Antiochians,[[4]](#cite_note-4) have slightly more strict rules, with fish only allowed on certain weekends.
These fasting rules are much the same as those observed during the Nativity Fast.

As with the three other fasting seasons of the church year, there is a [Great Feast](/wiki/Great_Feasts_of_the_Orthodox_Church "Great Feasts of the Orthodox Church") that may fall during the Apostles Fast; in this case, the Feast of the [Nativity of St. John the Baptist](/wiki/Nativity_of_St._John_the_Baptist "Nativity of St. John the Baptist") (June 24).

In some places, the Services on weekdays during the Apostles Fast are similar to the Services during [Great Lent](/wiki/Great_Lent "Great Lent") (with some variations). Many churches and monasteries in the Russian tradition will perform the Lenten Services on at least the first day of the Apostles Fast.

## Duration of the Fast

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Apostles%27_Fast&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Duration of the Fast")]

The length of the Fast is variable, being determined by the date of [Pascha](/wiki/Easter "Easter") (Easter). 8 weeks after Pascha comes the [Sunday of All Saints](/wiki/All_Saints_Day "All Saints Day"). The next day, Monday, the Fast of the Holy Apostles begins. The Fast lasts until June 29, the Feast of the Holy Apostles [Peter](/wiki/Saint_Peter "Saint Peter") and [Paul](/wiki/Paul_of_Tarsus "Paul of Tarsus"). Depending on the date of Pascha, the Apostles Fast can begin as early as May 18 or as late as June 21. It may be as short as eight days or as long as 42 days in duration.

The Apostles Fast may not be observed at all in certain years for those Orthodox using the [new calendar](/wiki/Revised_Julian_calendar "Revised Julian calendar") as the second Monday after Pentecost may fall on or after June 29, but it is always observed by the Orthodox using the traditional calendar. For those who follow the traditional [Julian calendar](/wiki/Julian_calendar "Julian calendar"), June 29 falls on the [Gregorian calendar](/wiki/Gregorian_calendar "Gregorian calendar") date of July 12.

## The Fast in Oriental traditions

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Apostles%27_Fast&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: The Fast in Oriental traditions")]

The [Coptic Orthodox Church](/wiki/Coptic_Orthodox_Church "Coptic Orthodox Church") fasts from the first Monday after [Pentecost](/wiki/Pentecost "Pentecost") until [4th Epip](/wiki/Coptic_calendar "Coptic calendar") according to the [Coptic calendar](/wiki/Coptic_calendar "Coptic calendar"), after which follows the [Apostles' Feast](/wiki/Feast_of_Saints_Peter_and_Paul "Feast of Saints Peter and Paul") day which is equivalent to [29th June](/wiki/29th_June "29th June") on the [Julian calendar](/wiki/Julian_calendar "Julian calendar").

The [Syriac Orthodox Church](/wiki/Syriac_Orthodox_Church "Syriac Orthodox Church") also fasted from the first Monday after [Pentecost](/wiki/Pentecost "Pentecost") until June 29, but the Council of Homs, held in 1946, shortened the fast by starting it on June 26.

In the [Maronite Church](/wiki/Maronite_Church "Maronite Church"), the duration of the fast has also been shortened as centuries passed by. Since the Maronite Church is an Oriental Church belonging to the [Syriac tradition](/wiki/Syriac_Christianity "Syriac Christianity"), we may suppose that in ancient times the fast used to start on first Monday after [Pentecost](/wiki/Pentecost "Pentecost"). In the 16th century, Maronites fasted approximately 30 days, even less in some regions.[[5]](#cite_note-5)

In 1598, the Synod of Dai'at Mussa shortened the fast to 14 days, making it start on June 15 (canon 30).[[6]](#cite_note-6) This decision was renewed at the Synod of Hrash, in 1644 (chapter 9, canon 5).[[7]](#cite_note-7) Finally, the [Lebanese Council of 1736](/wiki/Lebanese_Council_of_1736 "Lebanese Council of 1736") shortened the fast to 4 days, starting it on June 25 (part 1, chapter 4, canon 2). Maronites used to abstain from all animal products, including fish and sea products, and from wine, but did not abstain from oil.[[8]](#cite_note-8)

In the [Melkite Greek Catholic Church](/wiki/Melkite_Greek_Catholic_Church "Melkite Greek Catholic Church"), as well, the traditional start of the fast has been delayed. According to *Byzantine Daily Worship*, the fast begins on June 17. According to the liturgical calendars of the eparchies of [New Zealand, Australia](/wiki/Melkite_Greek_Catholic_Eparchy_of_Saint_Michael_Archangel_in_Sydney "Melkite Greek Catholic Eparchy of Saint Michael Archangel in Sydney"), as well as the [United States](/wiki/Melkite_Greek_Catholic_Eparchy_of_Newton "Melkite Greek Catholic Eparchy of Newton"), the fast begins on June 19.[[9]](#cite_note-9)
